DISCOVERY 610, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ASSIGNMENT OF RIGHTS, RESTRAINING ORDER, TURNOVER ORDER, AND TO APPEAR ON A WEEKLY BASIS - T. DEL BECCARO APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF AND BOB PERKISS APPEARING FOR RYAN, ET AL. ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. MOTION IS GRANTED. THE COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE MARCH 29, 2004 JUDGMENT CONFIRMING THE PREVIOUS ARBITRATION AWARD. THE JUDGMENT SPECIFIES IN PART A RECOVERY FROM MARK BORSUK AND LILIANE BORSUK IN THE AMOUNT OF $11,121.69 FOR UNPAID ASSESSMENTS THROUGH DECEMBER 31, 2002 AND ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS IN THE TOTAL SUM OF $46227.01, TOGETHER WITH INTEREST ON SAID SUMS AT THE RATE OF TEN PERCENT (10%) FROM APRIL 2, 2003. PETITIONERS THEREAFTER FILED A TIMELY NOTICE OF APPEAL FROM THE JUDGMENT. THE SUBSEQUENT AMENDED NOTICE OF APPEAL AND REQUEST FOR PARTIAL ABANDONMENT OR DISMISSAL OF SEVERABLE APPEAL DOES NOT CHANGE THE FACT THAT PETITIONERS HAVE APPEALED FROM THE JUDGMENT AND ARE THEREFORE SUBJECT TO THE REQUIREMENTS OF CCP SECTION 917.1. PETITIONERS' CONTENTION THAT THEY MAY STAY EXECUTION ON THE JUDGMENT BY TENDERING PARTIAL PAYMENT OF THE JUDGMENT WHILE PURSUING AN APPEAL AS DESCRIBED IN ZIELLO V. SUPERIOR COURT (1999) 75 CAL.APP.4TH 651 IS UNAVAILING. ZIELLO INVOLVED A JUDGMENT DEBTOR WHO PAID AND DID NOT APPEAL THE AMOUNT OF A JUDGMENT FOR DAMAGES, BUT WHO DID APPEAL A SEPARATE ORDER OF THE TRIAL COURT, AFTER JUDGMENT, ASSESSING COSTS AND ATTORNEY'S FEES. CCP SECTION 1003 DEFINES AN ORDER AS, "EVERY DIRECTION OF A COURT OR JUDGE, MADE OR ENTERED IN WRITING, AND NOT INCLUDED IN A JUDGMENT." IN ZIELLO, THE APPEAL DID NOT RELATE TO THE UNDERLYING JUDGMENT BUT WAS LIMITED TO THE ORDER AWARDING COSTS AND ATTORNEY'S FEES. (610/BCHAN)
